tasks.task: Identify JavaScript methods for accessing array elements and objects properties
tasks.description: Understand the use of `slice` and `splice` methods in manipulating arrays. Also, identify if and function usage to create a function within an object.

tasks.task: Identify the difference between undeclared, undefined variables and throw error.
tasks.description: Understand how to handle undeclared and undefined variables in JavaScript with appropriate errors (throw).

tasks.task: Write a function using `let` keyword to define a variable outside the block scope.
tasks.description: Define a variable named ABC with value 100, within an if statement inside which create another variable abc and assign it to the same value of ABC. Finally, log the result of `typeOf(ABC)` in console.

tasks.task: Write code using ES6 Object Destructuring syntax.
tasks.description: Inside an object definition named obj with key-value pairs for XY Z and PQR, include a function inside which calls another function that logs the type of variable XYZ. Use destructuring to access values from different levels of the nested object.

tasks.task: Understand differences between `slice` and `splice` methods in arrays.
tasks.description: Write code demonstrating the difference between slice (which does not modify the original array) and splice (which modifies the original array). Use these methods to manipulate objects within an object.

tasks.task: Identify why `typeOf` XYZ is undefined when it should be a function.
tasks.description: Inside an if block, define variables ABC and XY Z. Assign the value of ABC to a variable abc within that same scope but with a different name. After defining these variables, call a typeOf method on both variables (ABC and XY Z) in order to determine why `typeOf` is returning undefined for XYZ.

tasks.task: Understand usage and logic behind JavaScript find and includes methods.
tasks.description: Write code that uses the `includes` method on strings, including a second parameter (such as -2). Use this method to check if a string contains a specific substring. Also write code using the `find` method within an object structure.
